title: Why Zebras Don't Get Ulcers
subtitle: A Guide to Stress, Stress-Related Diseases, and Coping
author: Robert M. Sapolsky
excerpt: Why does the zebra not get ulcers? Because its stress response is acute — sprint from the lion, then graze. Humans activate the same physiology for months of mortgage anxiety. Robert Sapolsky explains what stress does to every system of the body, why chronic activation causes disease, and why social support, control, and predictability are biological necessities.
